maulgar.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (934) ran well below the Top 100 average (2,425) on this very short (37-second) burst-damage kill, and overheal (46.5%) sits close to the sample's single worst parse (47.3%, a 0.8-point gap) - still a decent 61st-percentile result, with 1 real death logged.
maulgar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: A real rank-choice gap shows up here: the Top 100 benchmark casts Chain Heal's highest real rank (guid 25423, 540 mana) 83.2% of the time on this boss, while Vajomee split it 51.9% high-rank / 19.7% at a cheaper lower rank (guid 10623, 405 mana) - the first of a pattern that repeats on every kill this raid night.
maulgar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched the Top 100 sample, though most (Earth Shield, Mana Tide Totem) see low real usage on this boss regardless (8-10% of the sample). The one real death (Pointsetta) lands nearest an Ancestral Swiftness cast 10.9 seconds later - a party buff, not a save.
maulgar.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (96.4% vs. 87.5% average) ran above the Top 100 norm, with top-target concentration (56.3% vs. 44.5% average, +11.8) also running higher than typical, across 9 distinct targets.

gruul.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (825) ran below the Top 100 average (1,558), and active time (80.5% vs. 91.5% average, a real 11-point gap) was the more notable issue - a strong 81st-percentile kill, the second-best of the raid night, with 1 real death logged.
gruul.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Vajomee's Chain Heal rank split (76.1% high-rank guid 25423 / 16.7% low-rank guid 10623) tracks closer to the Top 100 benchmark's own split (77.6% high-rank) than on any other kill this raid night - the one boss where this pattern nearly matches the norm.
gruul.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Mana Potion went uncast against a 57%-used Top 100 norm - the one real cooldown gap. The one real death (Vinnyvoss) lands nearest a Mana Tide Totem cast 35.8 seconds later - a party buff, not a targeted save.
gruul.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (51.3% vs. 59.8% average) and top-target concentration (12% vs. 19.8% average) both tracked modestly below the Top 100 norm, across 17 distinct targets.

hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (642) ran below the Top 100 average (1,471), and overheal (58.4%) sits close to the sample's single worst parse (60.4%, a 2-point gap) - a middling 50th-percentile kill, with 2 real deaths logged. This is also the first of two kills this raid night with no Well Fed food buff active, despite both elixirs being up.
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The Top 100 benchmark casts Chain Heal's highest rank (guid 25423) 68.5% of the time here, while Vajomee ran the opposite way - 52.8% at the cheaper lower rank (guid 10623) versus only 36.2% at the high rank - one of the larger rank-choice gaps of the raid night.
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Ancestral Swiftness went uncast against an 85%-used Top 100 norm - the one real cooldown gap. Neither real death (Staubz, Deepbowlz) lands close enough to a cooldown cast at them specifically to call a real save.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (71.1% vs. 77.5% average) tracked modestly below the Top 100 norm, while top-target concentration (25.5% vs. 47.7% average, -22.2) ran well below typical, across 15 distinct targets.

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (983) ran below the Top 100 average (1,971), but overheal (23.3%) ran a real 23 points better than the sample's single worst parse (46.3%) - the best overheal efficiency of the raid night - a strong 71st-percentile kill, with 9 real deaths logged, the most of any kill this raid night. This is also the only kill with no Battle or Guardian Elixir active at all (food only) - a real, confirmed consumable gap.
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The Top 100 benchmark runs 87.2% on Chain Heal's highest rank (guid 25423) here - the strongest benchmark preference for the high rank of any kill this raid night - while Vajomee split 52.6% high-rank / 37.4% at the cheaper lower rank (guid 10623).
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched the Top 100 sample. Only one real Mana Potion cast was logged, so it registers as the 'nearest' cooldown to all 9 deaths purely by default (5.6-26.5 seconds removed) - not a real save for any of them, and self-only besides.
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (49.1% vs. 51.2% average) and top-target concentration (14.6% vs. 14% average) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across 20 distinct targets.

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1,014) ran below the Top 100 average (2,159), with both target coverage (57.8% vs. 74% average, -13.4) and top-target concentration (21.7% vs. 34.5% average, -12.8) running real gaps below the Top 100 norm - a middling 54th-percentile kill, ranking 1st of the tracked healers present by item-level rank, with 2 real deaths logged.
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The Top 100 benchmark casts Chain Heal's highest rank (guid 25423) 84.3% of the time here, while Vajomee split it nearly evenly - 43.2% high-rank versus 38.3% at the cheaper lower rank (guid 10623) - a real, sizeable rank-choice gap.
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched the Top 100 sample. Neither real death (Staubz, Zuggnuggs) lands close enough to a cooldown cast at them specifically to call a real save.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage and top-target concentration both ran real gaps below the Top 100 norm (see scorecard), across 22 distinct targets.

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (815) ran below the Top 100 average (1,532), and active time (86.4% vs. 97.1% average, a real 10.7-point gap) was a real issue - a middling 55th-percentile kill, with 3 real deaths logged. This is the second kill this raid night with no Well Fed food buff active, despite both elixirs being up.
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The Top 100 benchmark casts Chain Heal's highest rank (guid 25423) 68.7% of the time here, while Vajomee ran the opposite way - 53.3% at the cheaper lower rank (guid 10623) versus only 34.9% at the high rank - one of the larger rank-choice gaps of the raid night.
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched the Top 100 sample. None of the 3 real deaths (Shlicktree, and Deepbowlz/Vinnyvoss who died within 1 millisecond of each other) land close enough to a cooldown cast at them specifically to call a real save.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (52.7% vs. 51.9% average) and top-target concentration (24.1% vs. 22.1% average) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across 26 distinct targets - the widest spread of the raid night.

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (694) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,759, a 39% ratio), landing at the 40th percentile, with zero real deaths logged.
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The Top 100 benchmark runs 74.3% on Chain Heal's highest rank (guid 25423) here, while Vajomee split 56.3% high-rank / 25.2% at the cheaper lower rank (guid 10623).
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Mana Potion went uncast against a 67%-used Top 100 norm - the one real cooldown gap this kill. No deaths to answer.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (51.2% vs. 52.1% average) and top-target concentration (13.5% vs. 16.8% average) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across 23 distinct targets, on a clean kill.

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (519) ran below the Top 100 average (1,085), and active time (76.2% vs. 94.2% average, a real 18-point gap) was a real issue on the raid's longest kill this night (543 seconds), landing at the 30th percentile, one of the raid's three lowest kills, with 6 real deaths logged, including Vajomee's own. Danceswtrees, a Restoration Druid also present in this same raid, died on this same kill as well.
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: This is the most extreme Chain Heal rank split of the entire raid night: Vajomee cast the cheaper lower rank (guid 10623, 405 mana) 83.7% of the time, versus only 7.4% at the highest rank (guid 25423, 540 mana) that the Top 100 benchmark itself favors 48.4% of the time here - a real, sizeable divergence from the benchmark's own rank mix on this specific fight.
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Dark Rune went uncast against a real 50%-used norm here - the highest baseline Dark Rune usage of any boss this raid night, and a real gap. Every other tracked cooldown matched the sample (Earth Shield cast 3 times, Mana Tide Totem twice). Vajomee's own death and Danceswtrees's both land 21-25 seconds from the nearest logged cooldown (both self- or other-targeted, not aimed at either of them) - not a confirmed save. The final 3 deaths (Purehentai, Staubz, Deepbowlz) cluster within about 1 second of each other near the end of the fight, nearest a single Mana Tide Totem cast - a shared burst event, not individual healing failures.
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (44% vs. 46.8% average) and top-target concentration (11.4% vs. 17.5% average) both tracked modestly below the Top 100 norm, across 25 distinct targets.

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (307) ran far below the Top 100 average (1,121, a 27% ratio - the weakest of the entire raid night), and overheal (64.4%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (50.6%) by 13.8 points - the largest overheal gap of the raid night - landing at the 9th percentile, by far the lowest of the raid night, though with zero real deaths logged. HPM (2.57, a 39% ratio) is also the weakest mana efficiency of the raid night.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The Top 100 benchmark casts Chain Heal's highest rank (guid 25423) 55.1% of the time here, while Vajomee split it 22.2% high-rank versus 41.9% at the cheaper lower rank (guid 10623) - a real, sizeable rank-choice gap, on the kill with this raid night's single weakest scorecard.
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Ancestral Swiftness went uncast against a real 97%-used Top 100 norm here - one of the highest baseline usage rates of any cooldown this raid night, and a real, notable gap. Earth Shield was cast 5 times (Jotes x4, Turbarct), the heaviest use of the raid night, in line with the sample. No real deaths to answer.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (74% vs. 54.5% average, +19.5) ran well above the Top 100 norm, with top-target concentration (30.8% vs. 23.6% average) tracking close to typical, across 19 distinct targets.

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1,399) ran below the Top 100 average (2,650), landing at the 38th percentile, one of the raid's three lowest kills, with 2 real deaths logged.
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: One of the largest Chain Heal rank splits of the raid night: the Top 100 benchmark runs 83.9% on the highest rank (guid 25423) here, while Vajomee ran the opposite way - 83.2% at the cheaper lower rank (guid 10623) versus only 15.9% at the high rank.
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Ancestral Swiftness went uncast against an 89%-used Top 100 norm - the one real cooldown gap. Neither real death (Captinspanky, Staubz) lands close enough to a cooldown cast at them specifically to call a real save.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (37.1% vs. 42.7% average) and top-target concentration (8.7% vs. 11% average) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across 24 distinct targets.

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (632) ran below the Top 100 average (1,654), and overheal (60%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (56.7%) by 3.3 points - a middling 45th-percentile kill, with 1 real death logged.
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Vajomee split Chain Heal almost exactly evenly here (47.9% high-rank guid 25423 / 48.3% low-rank guid 10623), while the Top 100 benchmark still favors the high rank more heavily (67.2%) - a real, if more moderate, version of this raid night's broader rank-choice pattern.
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched the Top 100 sample. The one real death (Captinspanky) lands nearest an Ancestral Swiftness cast 10.1 seconds later - a party buff, not a targeted save.
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (39.2% vs. 51.3% average, -12.1) ran a real gap below the Top 100 norm, while top-target concentration (10.4% vs. 15.3% average) tracked modestly below typical, across 24 distinct targets.

kaelthas.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (433) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,029), and active time (65.9% vs. 86.6% average, a real 20.7-point gap - the largest active-time gap of the raid night) stands out - landing at the 18th percentile, one of the raid's three lowest kills, with 6 real deaths logged, including Vajomee's own, which plausibly explains a real share of the reduced activity on this long (487-second) fight.
kaelthas.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Another large Chain Heal rank split: the Top 100 benchmark runs 61.7% on the highest rank (guid 25423) here, while Vajomee ran 66.9% at the cheaper lower rank (guid 10623) versus only 20.8% at the high rank.
kaelthas.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched the Top 100 sample (Earth Shield cast 4 times, the heaviest use alongside Alar). Nameitis and Vajomee's own death land within about 2 seconds of each other, both nearest the same Mana Tide Totem cast 15-17 seconds later - a party buff, not a save. Kilsby's death lands nearest an Ancestral Swiftness cast 5.3 seconds later, also a party buff. The remaining 3 deaths (Churbert, Staubz, Morguesmpson) land 56-186 seconds from the nearest logged cooldown - too far removed to call a save.
kaelthas.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (42.7% vs. 48.5% average) and top-target concentration (12.3% vs. 17.9% average) both tracked modestly below the Top 100 norm, across 25 distinct targets.

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: one real mid-raid gear change (Feet, still unenchanted after), plus one likely-benign gear variance (MainHand's temporary enchant, differing on 2 kills)
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: The Feet slot swaps from item 28752 (Maulgar through Vashj) to a different item, 30066 (Alar through Kael'thas) - a real mid-raid gear upgrade, but neither item carries a permanent enchant, so this remains a real, unresolved gap across the whole raid night. The Back slot (item 28765) also carries no permanent enchant on every kill - the same persistent gap seen on Vajomee's other recent raid nights.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: All 12 real bosses attempted were killed this raid night, including the Gruul's Lair pair (High King Maulgar, Gruul the Dragonkiller). This was a genuinely mixed raid night: Vajomee ranked 1st of the tracked healers present by item-level rank on only 2 of 12 kills (46% average rank percentile) and 1st by raw healing on just 1 of 12 - a notably weaker showing than Vajomee's other recent reports. Percentiles ranged widely, from a very low 9th (Al'ar, the lowest of any kill in any Vajomee report this session) to 81st (Gruul the Dragonkiller) - Al'ar, Kael'thas (18th), and Lady Vashj (30th) were the raid's three lowest kills. The single most consistent real pattern across all 12 kills: the Top 100 benchmark favors Chain Heal's highest real rank (guid 25423, 540 mana) on every boss (55-87% of parses, depending on the fight), while Vajomee cast meaningfully more of the cheaper lower rank (guid 10623, 405 mana) on every single kill - from a near-match on Gruul (76.1% high-rank vs. the benchmark's 77.6%) to an almost complete reversal on Lady Vashj (7.4% high-rank vs. the benchmark's 48.4%) and Void Reaver (15.9% vs. 83.9%). Two real self-deaths occurred this raid night (Lady Vashj, Kael'thas), and deaths were otherwise heaviest on Leotheras (9, the most of the raid night). The Lurker Below and Al'ar were the only two clean kills. Leotheras was the one kill with no Battle or Guardian Elixir active at all; Hydross and Morogrim were separately missing the Well Fed food buff despite having both elixirs up.
RaidOverview.RAID_WARNING_BANNER: On every single kill this raid night, Vajomee cast a meaningfully larger share of Chain Heal's cheaper, lower rank than the Top 100 benchmark does - reaching a near-total reversal on Lady Vashj (83.7% low-rank) and Void Reaver (83.2% low-rank). This is a real, mechanically-confirmed rank-choice pattern (verified by each guid's own real mana cost), not a composition quirk - worth a real look at rank selection during high-mana-pressure fights. See each boss's own page for the exact real numbers.

GearCheckItems.Description: MainHand's temporary enchant varies on two kills
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: The weapon and its permanent enchant are unchanged all raid night - only the temporary (weapon oil/stone) enchant differs on Leotheras (missing) and Kael'thas (a different real enchant, guid 2639), consistent with normal re-application timing rather than a gearing gap.
